tests.name: Blood Film (Peripheral Blood Smear)
tests.specimen: Whole blood / 3 mL or prepared slides
tests.notes: Examines blood cell morphology for abnormalities like anemia or infections. Manual review for atypical findings.

tests.name: Blood Group (ABO and Rh Factor)
tests.notes: ABO blood group and Rh (D) antigen typing.

tests.name: Clotting Profile (Coagulation Panel)
tests.specimen: Plasma / 2.7 mL per tube (citrated plasma); serum if additional tests
tests.container: Light blue-top (sodium citrate) tube; red-top for serum
tests.transport: Room temperature / Stable 24 hours; frozen for extended storage
tests.notes: Assesses blood clotting factors for bleeding or thrombotic disorders. Often includes PT/INR and aPTT; comprehensive profiles may add Thrombin Time or D-Dimer.

tests.name: D-Dimer
tests.notes: VTE rule-out in appropriate pretest probability.

tests.name: Hemoglobin Concentration (HB)
tests.notes: Hemoglobin level measurement. Often included in CBC.

tests.name: Hemoglobin Genotype
tests.notes: Hemoglobin electrophoresis for genotype (AA, AS, SS, SC, etc.).

tests.name: Malaria Parasite (Blood Film for MP)
tests.transport: Room temperature / Must be tested within 6 hours
tests.notes: Thick and thin blood films for malaria parasite detection.

tests.name: Packed Cell Volume (PCV / Hematocrit)
tests.notes: Hematocrit measurement. Often included in CBC.

tests.name: Type and Screen
tests.notes: ABO/Rh and antibody screen for surgery.

tests.name: Antistreptolysin O (ASO); titer
tests.notes: ASO titer for detection of recent streptococcal infection. Normal: <200 IU/mL (adults), <170 IU/mL (children). Elevated titers indicate recent Group A streptococcal infection.

tests.name: Blood Culture
tests.specimen: Whole blood / 8-10 mL per bottle (adult); 1-4 mL pediatric
tests.container: Aerobic and anaerobic blood culture bottles
tests.notes: Cultures blood for aerobic and anaerobic bacteria to detect bacteremia or septicemia. If positive, additional ID and susceptibility testing.

tests.name: Helicobacter pylori Test
tests.notes: Serology or stool antigen per lab method.

tests.name: Hepatitis B Profile
tests.notes: Panel includes HBsAg, HBsAb, HBeAg, HBeAb, HBcAb. HBsAg can be ordered separately.

tests.name: Hepatitis B Surface Antigen (HBsAg)
tests.notes: HBsAg screening. Can be ordered alone or as part of Hepatitis B Profile.

tests.name: Hepatitis C Virus Antibody (Anti-HCV)
tests.notes: HCV antibody screening test. Reflex to HCV RNA if positive.

tests.name: High Vaginal Swab (HVS)
tests.notes: Qualitative reporting for flora/pathogens; consider NAAT panel when indicated.

tests.name: HIV Screening (HIV 1 & 2 Antibodies)
tests.notes: HIV-1/HIV-2 antibody screen. Requires consent and counseling.

tests.name: Pregnancy Test (hCG)
tests.notes: Qualitative hCG test. Urine or serum. Can detect pregnancy early.

tests.name: RPR (Syphilis Screen)
tests.notes: Rapid plasma reagin; reflex confirmatory if positive.

tests.name: Sputum MCS (Microscopy, Culture, and Sensitivity)
tests.specimen: Sputum / 2 mL (early morning deep cough sample)
tests.transport: Room temperature; refrigerate if delayed / Stable 24-48 hours
tests.notes: Cultures sputum for bacteria with Gram stain and sensitivity if positive. For diagnosing lower respiratory infections like pneumonia.

tests.name: Stool MCS (Stool Culture and Sensitivity)
tests.transport: Refrigerated / Transport within 2 hours or use transport medium
tests.notes: Culture for enteric pathogens with sensitivity testing as indicated. Includes qualitative pathogen reporting.

tests.name: Tuberculosis Screening (TB)
tests.notes: AFB smear/culture or NAAT per protocol.

tests.name: Urine MCS (Urine Culture and Sensitivity)
tests.transport: Refrigerated / Must be cultured within 2 hours or preserved
tests.notes: Culture for bacterial identification and antibiotic susceptibility testing. Midstream clean catch preferred. Colony count ≥10^5 CFU/mL indicates significant bacteriuria.

tests.name: VDRL (Syphilis Screening)
tests.notes: Venereal Disease Research Laboratory test for syphilis screening.

tests.name: Widal Test (Typhoid Fever Serology)
tests.notes: Agglutination test for Salmonella typhi and paratyphi antibodies.

tests.name: Antinuclear Antibody (ANA)
tests.notes: Autoimmune screening; reflex pattern if positive.

tests.name: Specific IgE, Allergen Panel
tests.notes: Allergen-specific IgE; select panel or single allergen.

tests.name: Free Thyroxine (Free T4)
tests.notes: Thyroid function; often reflexed from abnormal TSH.

tests.name: 2-Hour Postprandial Glucose
tests.notes: Blood drawn 2 hours after meal. Normal <140 mg/dL (<7.8 mmol/L). Used for diabetes screening and monitoring.

tests.name: Basic Metabolic Panel (BMP)
tests.container: Serum separator tube (SST) or red-top tube
tests.notes: Includes glucose, electrolytes, BUN, creatinine, calcium.

tests.name: Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CMP)
tests.notes: Includes BMP plus liver function tests (ALT, AST, etc.).

tests.name: Electrolytes, Urea, and Creatinine (EUC) Test
tests.notes: Includes Sodium, Potassium, Chloride, Bicarbonate, Urea, and Creatinine.

tests.name: Estrogen (E2)
tests.notes: Reference ranges vary by sex, menstrual phase, and reproductive status.

tests.name: Fasting Blood Sugar (FBS)
tests.notes: Fasting required (8-12 hours). Also known as Fasting Blood Glucose.

tests.name: Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH)
tests.notes: Reference ranges vary by sex and menstrual phase.

tests.name: Hormonal Profile (Panel)
tests.notes: Panel includes Prolactin, Testosterone (Total), FSH, LH, Estrogen (E2), and Progesterone. Prefer morning collection for testosterone.

tests.name: Lipid Panel
tests.notes: Fasting preferred. Measures cholesterol, HDL, LDL, triglycerides.

tests.name: Luteinizing Hormone (LH)
tests.notes: Reference ranges vary by sex and menstrual phase.

tests.name: Progesterone
tests.notes: Reference ranges vary by menstrual phase or pregnancy.

tests.name: Prolactin
tests.notes: Reference ranges vary by sex and pregnancy status.

tests.name: Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA)
tests.notes: For males; screening for prostate issues.

tests.name: PSA, Free
tests.notes: Prostate cancer risk stratification with total PSA.

tests.name: Random Blood Sugar (RBS)
tests.notes: No fasting required. Random glucose level.

tests.name: Testosterone (Total)
tests.notes: Prefer morning draw. Reference ranges vary by sex and age.

tests.name: Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH)
tests.notes: Reflex to Free T4 if abnormal (additional code if reflexed).

tests.name: Uric Acid Test
tests.notes: For gout monitoring and kidney function assessment.

tests.name: Urinalysis (UA)
tests.notes: Complete UA; reflex to microscopic if indicated.
